items.title: Better Consumer Staples ETF: State Street's XLP vs. Fidelity's FSTA
items.author: Robert Izquierdo
items.description: Fidelity's FSTA and State Street's XLP are both defensive consumer staples ETFs with identical 0.08% expense ratios. XLP offers higher dividend yield (2.7% vs 2.3%), greater liquidity, and concentrated exposure to 36 large-cap companies, while FSTA provides broader diversification with 104 holdings. The choice depends on whether investors prioritize income and liquidity (XLP) or diversification (FSTA).

insights.sentiment_reasoning: XLP is highlighted for superior liquidity ($14.9B AUM), higher dividend yield (2.7%), 27-year track record, and focused exposure to large-cap consumer staples leaders like Walmart and Costco, making it attractive for income-focused investors.

FSTA is presented as a solid alternative offering broader diversification with 104 holdings versus XLP's 36, providing exposure to a wider range of consumer staples companies while maintaining the same low 0.08% expense ratio.
Walmart is mentioned as a top holding in both ETFs, representing a key large-cap consumer staples company, but no specific performance or outlook commentary is provided.
Costco is identified as a major top holding in both ETFs, but serves only as a reference point for portfolio composition without additional sentiment indicators.
Procter & Gamble is listed as a top holding in both ETFs but is mentioned only for portfolio composition purposes without performance or outlook commentary.
